role_title: Senior Product Manager, Project & Task Experience

location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in office; Wednesday typically WFH; Friday depends on role

secondary_archetype: Technical PM (enterprise B2B, complex data models)

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in Product Management
Expected to mentor others and share PM best practices
Comp range $202K-$230K base, consistent with Senior PM at a large tech company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in Product Management.
Systems thinker: You feel just as comfortable modeling data relationships in a technical diagram, just as well as you can partner with Design to walk stakeholders through a holistic UX flow.
You create, flex, and evangelize a roadmap; thoughtfully break down projects to MVPs and iterative projects to ship with high velocity and business impact.
You analyze our top customer personas; dig into the roots of customer needs; synthesize research to gain a deep understanding and narrow definition of a problem.

You partner with cross-functional teammates to deliver high-quality results. You speak with clarity and write sharp product specs. You are highly comfortable getting into deep technical discussions with engineers about the pros and cons of different approaches, and you pair with design on complex user flows.
You are a mentor for others; create great team morale among other functions and PMs; and share PM best practices you've cultivated to date.
Maximize clarity by bringing a data-driven mindset to setting concrete goals and milestones
Demonstrates curiosity about AI tools and emerging technologies, with a willingness to learn and leverage them to enhance productivity, collaboration, or decision-making

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in building for complex data models and visualizations, human-centric UX, and B2B platform or enterprise experience preferred.

scope.surface: Project & Task Experience — core project and task management product features for enterprise customers
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / enterprise work management platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Adoption of new project and task features
core_responsibilities: Define and deliver a compelling roadmap that delivers value for enterprise-level customers
Partner closely with Product Design to create an enterprise software experience that's intuitive and dead simple
Create a product strategy to balance multiple priorities and use cases across customers, product teams, and enterprise-level compliance
Maximize clarity by bringing a data-driven mindset to setting concrete goals and milestones
Partner with engineering, design, and other functions to execute on roadmaps with high velocity

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as additional component of compensation package

green_flags: Explicit encouragement to apply even if not every requirement is met
Clear product surface and ownership area (Project & Task Experience)
Yearly pay equity audit demonstrates compensation fairness commitment
red_flags: Hard hybrid requirement in NYC (Mon/Tue/Thu in office) — eliminates remote candidates
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in JD
Friday WFH policy described as ambiguous ('depends on the type of work you do')

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, years, comp, and mentorship expectations)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and 'enterprise' language)
ambiguities: The AI curiosity requirement ('Demonstrates curiosity about AI tools...') is listed unconditionally in the 'About you' section but uses softer language ('curiosity', 'willingness to learn') compared to other hard requirements. Classified as hard given its unconditional placement.
Friday WFH policy is described conditionally ('depends on the type of work you do and the teams with which you partner'), making exact in-office expectations unclear without recruiter clarification.

The 'B2B platform or enterprise experience preferred' clause is attached to the 5+ years experience bullet, making it ambiguous whether it modifies the full requirement or is a standalone preference. Treated as nice-to-have given the word 'preferred'.
